title: Sri Parashakthi Kshetra
tagline: Sri Parashakthi Kshetra — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Sri Parashakthi Kshetra (Kannada: ಶ್ರೀ ಪರಾಶಕ್ತಿ ಕ್ಷೇತ್ರ, ಮಡ್ಯಾರು), a temple complex at Madyar, Kotekar.
overview.description: Sri Parashakthi Kshetra (Kannada: ಶ್ರೀ ಪರಾಶಕ್ತಿ ಕ್ಷೇತ್ರ, ಮಡ್ಯಾರು), a temple complex at Madyar, Kotekar. Madyar, located around 20 km from Mangalore, the port city of Karnataka, India where the Hindu goddess Vaishnavi is worshiped as the presiding deity.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kotekara
topPlaces.description: Kotekara is a census town in Dakshina Kannada district in the Indian state of Karnataka.Kotekar is located on NH-17 south of Mangalore towards Talapady.

topPlaces.name: Kotekar
topPlaces.description: Kotekar is a Town in Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka state, India. It is on the way from Mangalore to Talapady.

topPlaces.name: NITTE
topPlaces.description: NITTE (Deemed to be University) is an institute of higher education and a private deemed university located in Deralakatte, Mangaluru, Karnataka, India. It was established under the Nitte Education Trust, which has founded 31 educational institutions across three campuses in Nitte, Mangaluru, and Bengaluru.

topPlaces.name: Deralakatte
topPlaces.description: Deralakatte (University town) - is a major educational, healthcare, commercial and residential locality in the south-eastern part of Mangalore City in the Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka state. It is 9 km (5.6 mi) away from the Karnataka-Kerala state border.

topPlaces.name: Manjanady
topPlaces.description: Manjanady is a village in the Indian state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Mangalore taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Munnur
topPlaces.description: Munnur is a census town in Dakshina Kannada district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Kinnya
topPlaces.description: Kinnya or Kinya is a village in Ullal taluk, Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka state, India. The ancient Talapady Durga Parameshwari Temple is in this village.

topPlaces.name: Thokottu
topPlaces.description: Thokottu is a commercial junction to the south of Mangalore city on National Highway 66 (previously known as NH 17) and the gateway to Mangalore city from the South (Kerala). It is one of the busiest junctions of Mangalore city where the Medical Corridor Road goes towards east to Deralakatte and Konaje, educational hubs and the other road towards west goes to Ullal.

It was established under the Nitte Education Trust, which has founded 31 educational institutions across three campuses in Nitte, Mangaluru, and Bengaluru.

Thokottu is a commercial junction to the south of Mangalore city on National Highway 66 (previously known as NH 17) and the gateway to Mangalore city from the South (Kerala). It is one of the busiest junctions of Mangalore city where the Medical Corridor Road goes towards east to Deralakatte and Konaje, educational hubs and the other road towards west goes to Ullal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Parashakthi Kshetra?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Parashakthi Kshetra is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Parashakthi Kshetra?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (8 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Parashakthi Kshetra?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Parashakthi Kshetra at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Parashakthi Kshetra?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Parashakthi Kshetra start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Parashakthi Kshetra?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kotekara, Kotekar, NITTE, Deralakatte, Manjanady.
